title: Generate and implement WebGPU demo 7/10
description:
## Task
Generate and implement a new WebGPU demo (demo 7 of 10 in this batch). Follow the spec at docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md.

### Process
1. Read docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md for accumulated knowledge
2. Check src/lib/registry.ts for all existing demos
3. Choose ONE unexplored WebGPU/TSL capability (different from demos 1-6)
4. Generate concept with distinct name, title, description, accent color
5. Create src/demos/<name>/index.tsx
6. Add entry to registry.ts
7. pnpm build — zero errors
8. Verify render via Playwright
9. Save thumbnail
10. Update learnings.md

### Quality Gate
2 attempts max, simplify on attempt 2, skip on failure.

### Test Steps
1. pnpm build zero errors
2. Demo renders visually
3. Thumbnail saved
4. Registry entry with distinct color
5. Learnings updated
6. Different capability than demos 1-6

## Execution
1. bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"
2. bd close <your-id>

## Capturing Gaps
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"

## Capturing Learnings
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"

comments.text: [summary] Created bloom-orbs: 5 glowing orbs with layered halo shells simulating bloom via BackSide+AdditiveBlending. Targets bloom/glow post-processing via TSL.

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [summary] Created volumetric-cloud: 8 nested icosahedron shells with additive blending creating nebula volumetric effect. Key learning: very low per-shell opacity (0.015-0.04) needed for 8+ additive shells, BackSide > DoubleSide.

title: Generate and implement WebGPU demo 10/10
description:
## Task
Generate and implement the FINAL WebGPU demo (demo 10 of 10 in this batch). Follow the spec at docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md.

### Process
1. Read docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md for accumulated knowledge from ALL previous demos
2. Check src/lib/registry.ts for all existing demos
3. Choose the LAST unexplored WebGPU/TSL capability (different from demos 1-9)
4. Generate concept with distinct name, title, description, accent color
5. Create src/demos/<name>/index.tsx
6. Add entry to registry.ts
7. pnpm build — zero errors
8. Verify render via Playwright
9. Save thumbnail
10. Update learnings.md with final patterns

### Final Batch Summary
After completing this demo, also:
- Append a Batch 1 Summary to docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md under 'Batch History'
- Include: demos created, demos skipped, key learnings, total demo count

### Quality Gate
2 attempts max, simplify on attempt 2, skip on failure.

### Test Steps
1. pnpm build zero errors
2. Demo renders visually
3. Thumbnail saved
4. Registry entry with distinct color
5. Learnings updated with final patterns
6. Batch History summary appended to learnings.md
7. Different capability than demos 1-9

## Execution
1. bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done, plus batch summary>"
2. bd close <your-id>

## Capturing Gaps
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"

## Capturing Learnings
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"

title: Generate and implement WebGPU demo 1/10
description:
## Task
Generate and implement a new WebGPU demo (demo 1 of 10 in this batch). Follow the spec at docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md.

### Process
1. Read docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md for accumulated knowledge
2. Read the existing demo list in docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md to understand what's already covered
3. Choose ONE unexplored WebGPU/TSL capability from the target list in the spec
4. Generate a concept (name, title, description, accent color) that targets this capability
5. Create src/demos/<name>/index.tsx following the reference templates in the spec
6. Add entry to src/lib/registry.ts (set requiresWebGPU appropriately)
7. Run pnpm build — must compile with zero errors
8. Start pnpm dev, open http://localhost:5173/#<name> in browser (Playwright), screenshot to verify render
9. Save screenshot as public/thumbnails/<name>.jpg
10. Update docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md with working patterns, broken patterns, and visual quality notes

### Quality Gate
- Attempt 1: Fix errors based on error message
- Attempt 2: Simplify to tsl-torus pattern using only proven TSL nodes from learnings
- If still failing: skip, record failure in learnings, close this bead with reason

### Test Steps
1. pnpm build completes with zero errors
2. Demo renders visually (not black screen, not error)
3. Thumbnail saved to public/thumbnails/<name>.jpg
4. Registry entry added with all required fields
5. Learnings file updated with patterns from this demo

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"
2. Close the bead: bd close <your-id>

## Capturing Gaps
If you discover missing work that's clearly needed:
bd comments add <your-id> "[GAP] <title> - <description>"

## Capturing Learnings
If you encounter something noteworthy:
bd comments add <your-id> "[LEARNING] <description>"

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [summary] Created noise-dissolve demo: dodecahedron with hash-based dissolve effect using alphaTest/opacityNode, orange edge glow, teal fresnel rim. Targets advanced TSL noise functions (hash, smoothstep). Recorded learnings about If()/Discard() not working in useMemo context.

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [LEARNING] hash(positionLocal.mul(N)) produces concentric ring patterns on smooth geometry, not random dissolve. For true random dissolve, investigate UV-based noise or noise textures.

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [summary] Created uv-kaleidoscope: procedural mandala with polar UV folding, spherizeUV warping, animated concentric patterns. Targets TSL UV manipulation. Learned atan(y,x) vs .atan2(), spherizeUV(), uv() access.

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [summary] Created sprite-sparks: 600 instanced spark particles in spiral fountain with warm-to-cool gradient. Targets sprite/billboard particles. Key learning: PointsNodeMaterial broken in R3F/WebGPU, use instanced mesh instead.

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [LEARNING] PointsNodeMaterial renders nearly invisible in R3F/WebGPU regardless of settings. Always use instanced mesh approach for particles.

title: Generate and implement WebGPU demo 2/10
description:
## Task
Generate and implement a new WebGPU demo (demo 2 of 10 in this batch). Follow the spec at docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md.

### Process
1. Read docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md for accumulated knowledge from previous demos in this batch
2. Read the existing demo list in docs/ralph-specs/batch-demos.md AND check src/lib/registry.ts for any demos added by earlier tasks in this batch
3. Choose ONE unexplored WebGPU/TSL capability from the target list (different from demo 1)
4. Generate a concept (name, title, description, accent color distinct from all existing)
5. Create src/demos/<name>/index.tsx following the reference templates
6. Add entry to src/lib/registry.ts
7. Run pnpm build — must compile with zero errors
8. Verify render via Playwright screenshot
9. Save thumbnail to public/thumbnails/<name>.jpg
10. Update docs/ralph-specs/learnings.md

### Quality Gate
- Attempt 1: Fix errors based on error message
- Attempt 2: Simplify to tsl-torus pattern using only proven TSL nodes from learnings
- If still failing: skip, record failure in learnings, close with reason

### Test Steps
1. pnpm build completes with zero errors
2. Demo renders visually (not black screen, not error)
3. Thumbnail saved to public/thumbnails/<name>.jpg
4. Registry entry added with all required fields and distinct color
5. Learnings file updated with patterns from this demo
6. Concept targets a DIFFERENT capability than demo 1

## Execution
Execute this task directly. When complete:
1. Add a summary comment: bd comments add <your-id> "[summary] <what was done>"
2. Close the bead: bd close <your-id>

comments.author: Chris Nienhuis
comments.text: [summary] Created screen-hologram demo: holographic icosahedron with CRT scanlines, glitch bands, and fresnel rim using TSL screenUV. Learned screenUV for screen-fixed effects, fract() for repeating patterns.
